channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Was Flattering, Xi Was Resolute. The Difference Spoke Volumes.
item.description: In contrast to his rhetoric about China at home, President Trump spoke in conciliatory terms with Xi Jinping, the Chinese leader.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.media:description: President Trump with President Xi Jinping of China in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: In Photos and Video: President Trump Goes to China
item.description: His talks with Xi Jinping in Beijing could decide whether the United States and China maintain their uneasy truce on trade.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, visiting the Temple of Heaven in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Wes Streeting Resigns and Calls for Leadership Contest to Replace Starmer
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer now appears set to face at least two possible challengers: his health minister, who resigned Thursday, and the popular mayor of Manchester.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Wes Streeting resigned from his job as health minister on Thursday and called for a leadership contest against Mr. Starmer.

item.title: Cecilia Flores Became the Voice of Mexican Mothers
item.description: One of the most prominent activists for Mexico’s disappeared recently found the remains of one missing son. Now she has turned her attention to finding the other.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and Fred Ramos
item.media:credit: Fred Ramos for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cecilia Flores during a search for her missing son, Alejandro Guadalupe Islas Flores, in Juan José Ríos, Sinaloa, Mexico.

item.title: Key Moments From the First Day of Trump’s China Visit
item.description: During the first round of two days of talks, Xi Jinping issued a stern warning about Taiwan while Trump touted all the top business leaders in his delegation.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:description: President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, visiting the Temple of Heaven in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Inside the Secret Mission to Fly Taiwan’s President to Africa
item.description: From satellite phone check-ins to a borrowed royal plane, new details show how Taiwan’s leader’s team outwitted China and pulled off an audacious journey to southern Africa.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley and Amy Chang Chien
item.media:credit: Taiwan Presidential Office, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A photograph provided by the Taiwan Presidential Office showed the Taiwanese leader, Lai Ching-te, waving upon arriving in Eswatini in May.

item.title: Iran Lets Chinese Ships Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Rising Tensions After Vessel Seizure
item.description: Iranian news agencies said some Chinese ships were being allowed through the strait, following diplomatic outreach to Iran from Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz early this month.

item.title: Nigel Farage, Leader of Reform UK, Says £5 Million Gift Was ‘Reward’ for Brexit
item.description: Nigel Farage, the leader of the populist right-wing party, had previously said the money was for personal security.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Nigel Farage during a campaign event in London in April.

item.title: Saudi Arabia and U.A.E. Carried Out Secret Attacks in Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The Gulf Arab states have been grappling with how to deter Iran after the war made clear the limits of American security guarantees.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran, the capital of Iran, last week.

item.title: Duterte Ally Flees to Evade I.C.C. Arrest Warrant After Chaos at Philippine Senate
item.description: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, who is wanted by the International Criminal Court in The Hague, had been holed up in the building when shots rang out.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee and Jason Gutierrez
item.media:description: Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa answering questions at the Philippine Senate on Wednesday. He fled the building early on Thursday, and federal agents are looking for him, a senior official said.

item.title: Over 100 People Killed in India’s Uttar Pradesh as Storms Wreak Destruction
item.description: The deaths were scattered across a swath of India’s most populous state, Uttar Pradesh, where many homes were destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Hari Kumar and Nazaneen Ghaffar
item.media:description: Storm damage in Prayagraj, a city in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh, on Wednesday.

item.title: Argentina Races To Find Origin of Hantavirus Outbreak
item.description: The scientific investigation into the origin of cruise infection has become entangled with international finger pointing.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola
item.media:description: A sign shows information on hantavirus at a hospital in Bariloche, Argentina. A patient with no known connection to the cruise ship is hospitalized there with hantavirus.

item.title: Russia Pummels Kyiv in the Largest Drone Attack of the War
item.description: At least 12 people were killed and dozens were wounded in a drone and missile assault, days after President Vladimir Putin of Russia said the war could end soon.
item.dc:creator: Siobhán O’Grady and Oleksandra Mykolyshyn
item.media:description: A residential building hit by Russian strikes in Kyiv, Ukraine, on Thursday.

item.title: Judge Orders U.S. to Return Colombian Woman Deported to Congo
item.description: The unusual ruling came after the judge found that the Trump administration had most likely violated the law by deporting the 55-year-old woman to the African country despite its refusal to take her.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:description: The Colombian woman and other migrants are housed in a hotel outside Kinshasa, the capital of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: 16 More People in the U.S. Are Being Monitored for Hantavirus, C.D.C. Says
item.description: They were passengers on a plane to Johannesburg with an infected Dutch woman who later died.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: A person in a biohazard suit at the airport in Omaha, Neb., as passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius arrived on Monday.

item.title: Glitz and Boycotts at Eurovision
item.description: The song contest has camp, glitter and pyrotechnics. And, this year, protests over Israel’s participation.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: Cuba Says It Has Run Out of Oil
item.description: A long-running energy crisis in the island nation reached a breaking point this week, when government officials said they had run out of reserves.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles
item.media:credit: Lisette Poole for The New York Times
item.media:description: A gas station in Havana this month. In January, the Trump administration cut off oil shipments from Venezuela, which had been supplying a large amount of Cuba’s oil.

item.title: Trump’s and Xi’s Body Language at the Summit Mirrored Their Styles
item.description: Although at odds over issues like trade and Taiwan, the U.S. and Chinese leaders met in Beijing with a show of friendly gestures.
item.dc:creator: Alan Yuhas

item.title: A Top U.S. Commander Dismisses Reports of Civilian Deaths in Iran
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper said a strike on an Iranian school may have been the only civilian casualty incident of the war.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Admiral Cooper told the lawmakers that his staff had been proactive in trying to prevent civilian deaths. “Our staff specifically warned the Iranian people more than 100 times about the threat of them being used as human shields,” he said.

item.title: Spies, Sanctions, Cyberattacks: China and the U.S. Clash Behind the Scenes
item.description: After months of avoiding confrontation, the Trump administration has taken recent steps to call out China on Iran, artificial intelligence and spying.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Dustin Volz
item.media:description: President Trump met with President Xi Jinping of China on Thursday after a flurry of actions from his administration in recent weeks against China.

item.title: Hantavirus Doesn’t Spread Easily, but Officials May Be Downplaying Risks
item.description: The virus is clearly far less contagious than the coronavirus, scientists agree, but they have found cases where it spread among people without direct contact.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Passengers from the MV Hondius evacuated from the cruise ship this week in Tenerife, Spain.

item.title: Xi Warned Trump About the ‘Thucydides Trap’ for U.S. and China. What Is It?
item.description: China’s leader reached for Greek history to warn the United States of what can happen when a rising power meets an incumbent one.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.media:credit: Eye Ubiquitous/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A statue of Thucydides, the Athenian general and historian, outside the parliament building in Vienna.

item.title: Trump’s Push to Keep Coal Plants Open Is Costing Hundreds of Millions
item.description: Costs have been mounting in the year since the Trump administration began directing aging coal plants to stay open.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown
item.media:credit: Jim West/UCG/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The J.H. Campbell coal-fired power plant in West Olive, Mich., one of five coal plants in the United States that President Trump ordered to remain open. The plant has reported $180 million in expenses associated with running the facility since last May.

item.title: Who Is Wes Streeting, the Cabinet Minister Who Just Resigned From Keir Starmer’s Government?
item.description: Mr. Streeting attributes his ability to connect with a range of voters to his background. He was the first in his family to graduate from college and his grandfather went to prison for armed robbery.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Wes Streeting has prided himself on a background that he said enables him to relate to many voters.

item.title: Angela Rayner, Former U.K. Deputy Prime Minister, Cleared of Wrongdoing Over Taxes
item.description: Angela Rayner, who resigned last year after paying the wrong rate of tax on an apartment purchase, said an investigation found she had not done so deliberately.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear and Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Angela Rayner last year. She has been seen as a potential successor to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: Who Attended the U.S.-China State Dinner in Beijing?
item.description: Guests included top U.S. and Chinese officials and the chief executives of several major American companies.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio attended the state dinner at the Great Hall of the People.

item.title: Who Owns These Artworks? Musée d’Orsay Hopes Visitors Can Help Find Out.
item.description: A new room in the Musée d’Orsay’s permanent display includes 13 pieces that were recovered from Germany and Austria after World War II and whose provenance is unknown.
item.dc:creator: Ségolène Le Stradic

item.title: Where Are the Women on Trump’s Visit to China?
item.description: Nearly all the business leaders and officials accompanying President Trump in China are men. There are few women on the Chinese side, too.
item.dc:creator: Emily McGarvey
item.media:description: President Xi Jinping of China greeting the U.S. delegation at the Great Hall of the People on Thursday.

item.title: What People in China Say of Trump
item.description: Residents in four Chinese cities described a mixture of amusement and anger, blaming U.S. tensions for a slowing economy and rising fuel prices.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:description: President Trump at the state banquet in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Invites Xi to White House During State Dinner in Beijing
item.description: President Trump and President Xi Jinping of China emphasized cooperation in their remarks after walking into the Great Hall of the People side by side.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Luke Broadwater
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping, China’s leader, emphasized their cooperation on Thursday.

item.title: China’s State Dinner Menu for Trump Includes Beef Ribs, Roast Duck and Tiramisu
item.description: A mixture of Chinese and international dishes were on the menu as Beijing’s chefs sought to appeal to President Trump’s tastes.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.media:description: President Trump during the State Banquet on Thursday.

item.title: What to Know About Day 1 of Trump and Xi’s Meeting
item.description: President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, exchanged pleasantries and also tackled areas of contention like Taiwan and trade.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: President Trump and China’s leader Xi Jinping at the state dinner on Thursday in Beijing.

item.title: Chinese News Media Cast Trump’s Visit as a Sign of Beijing’s Rising Stature
item.description: Chinese state media is depicting the summit as an opportunity for the United States to accept that the “right way” for the two powers to engage is as equals.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Berry Wang
item.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping, China’s leader, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Trump and Xi Discuss the Need to Open the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Says
item.description: Earlier, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said in an interview that China should take a more active role in resolving a standoff over the strait.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio departing the White House for a trip to China on Tuesday.

item.title: Elon Musk Returns to Trump’s Side in Beijing
item.description: Mr. Musk is part of a delegation of business leaders to China, where his interests include Tesla’s electric vehicle factory and solar panels.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and Meaghan Tobin
item.media:description: Elon Musk arriving in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Can Some Very Tiny Particles Cool the Planet? One Tech Company Says Yes.
item.description: Stardust Solutions says its tiny spheres can reflect the sun’s rays without harming people or the environment. Critics say private companies have no business altering Earth’s atmosphere.
item.dc:creator: Eric Niiler
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times

item.title: China Restores Beef Trade With U.S. as Goodwill Gesture for Trump Visit
item.description: Beijing approved beef shipment licenses for hundreds of American slaughterhouses, although it appeared to claw back many of them later in the day.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: Beijing granted permission on Thursday for hundreds of American slaughterhouses to resume beef shipments to China.

item.title: Xi referenced ‘great changes unseen in a century.’ Here’s what that means.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, said on Thursday that the world was undergoing a “once-in-a-century transformation.”

item.title: Xi Says U.S.-China Relations Could Turn ‘Dangerous’ Over Taiwan
item.description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, said relations between the two countries could enter an “extremely dangerous place” if Mr. Trump ignored Chinese demands over Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: Patriot missile systems on display during a Taiwanese military exercise in Taipei last year.

item.title: Inside Trump’s Summit Tour in Beijing: From Temple of Heaven to the Great Hall
item.description: The Temple of Heaven and Great Hall of the People embody distinct parts of China’s history, which Xi Jinping has sought to underscore while hosting state visits.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Joy Dong
item.category: Great Hall of the People (Beijing, China)
item.media:description: Mr. Trump and Mr. Xi at the Temple of Heaven in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Trump’s 2017 visit to Beijing offers clues for this year’s summit.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:description: President Trump, Xi Jinping and their wives at a Chinese opera in Beijing’s Forbidden City in 2017.

item.title: Beijing Tightens Security As Trump and Xi Meet in China
item.description: Residents of China’s capital went about their daily lives with minimal interruption as President Trump and Xi Jinping, China’s leader, met at historic sites.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Francesca Regalado and Murphy Zhao

item.title: Why Does China Want Taiwan?
item.description: There are few issues in diplomacy more complicated than the status of the self-governing island, which China claims as its own. It is almost certain to come up when President Trump meets China’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:credit: Lam Yik Fei for The New York Times
item.media:description: Taiwan is certain to be raised by China’s leader, Xi Jinping, when he hosts President Trump this week.

item.title: Activists, Lawmakers Urge Trump to Call for Release of Jimmy Lai and Dong Yuyu
item.description: Supporters of Mr. Lai, a Hong Kong dissident, and Mr. Dong, a Chinese journalist, hope President Trump will raise their cases with President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Lam Yik Fei for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jimmy Lai at his home in Hong Kong in 2020. He was sentenced in February to 20 years in prison after Beijing blamed the Hong Kong publisher for leading the city’s major protests nearly seven years ago.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo, David E. Sanger, Ana Swanson and Luke Broadwater

item.title: Canada and Alberta Reach Agreement to Raise Carbon Prices as Pipeline Tradeoff
item.description: But the agreement is the latest softening of carbon reduction measures introduced by Prime Minister Mark Carney since he took office last year.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Pat Kane for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Syncrude facility near Fort McMurray, Alberta, producing synthetic crude oil from oil sands.

item.title: The China Gambit: From Nixon to Trump
item.description: President Trump’s visit to Beijing comes as relations with China have become more combative.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.media:description: President Richard M. Nixon visiting Beijing in 1972.

item.title: As Trump and Xi Meet in China, American and Chinese People Are Drifting Apart
item.description: Past meetings between the presidents of the U.S. and China have involved friendly displays of cultural exchange. Those gestures have largely dried up.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:description: President Trump and the first lady, Melania Trump, with the Chinese leader, Xi Jinping, and his wife, Peng Liyuan, during a tour of the Forbidden City, in Beijing in 2017.

item.title: Black Bear Fatally Mauls Uranium Contractor in Northern Canada
item.description: The attack, at a remote uranium mining site in northern Saskatchewan, was only the fourth fatal black bear encounter in the province’s recorded history, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:description: The black bear is the most common species in Canada, though bear attacks are rare.

item.title: Why Xi Doesn’t Need a Deal With Trump
item.description: President Xi Jinping of China is buying time for Beijing and may see an opening with a U.S. president weakened by the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Xi Jinping of China, seen here in Beijing on Tuesday, has been seeking to redefine the terms of how China and the United States should engage.

item.title: Alberta Separation Petition Is Dealt a Court Blow
item.description: A judge sided with an Indigenous group that had asked for a petition by separatists to force a vote on the oil-rich province seceding from Canada to be thrown out.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Separatist leader Mitch Sylvestre is seen during a rally in front of the Elections Alberta headquarters in Edmonton, Canada, this month, as they submit boxes of signatures in the hope of triggering an independence referendum.

item.title: Trump in Beijing
item.description: The president is getting the red carpet treatment, but the deep rift between the U.S. and China will be very hard to repair.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: At the airport in Beijing for President Trump’s arrival.

item.title: Cynthia Shange, Who Defied Apartheid at a Beauty Pageant, Dies at 76
item.description: She was the first Black South African to enter the Miss World contest, placing in the top five in 1972.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.media:description: Cynthia Shange competing at a Miss World event in London in 1972. South Africa’s president praised the boldness of her ambitions “in an era when apartheid segregation was at its peak.”

item.title: Israel Says Netanyahu Made a Secret Trip to U.A.E., Which Says He Didn’t
item.description: The claim and the denial signaled both how close Israel and the United Arab Emirates have become, and how delicate that relationship remains.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Jerusalem in April.

item.title: More U.S. Spy Planes are Surveilling Cuba
item.description: As President Trump threatens to act against Cuba, experts are debating whether the flights, which the United States is not taking pains to hide, are just intimidation or foreshadow military force.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles, Eric Schmitt and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A taxi driver in Havana earlier this month.

item.title: Top Gaza Negotiator Urges Hamas to Embrace Rebuilding Plan
item.description: Nickolay Mladenov, who is overseeing the U.S.-led truce in Gaza, has tried to convince the militant group to give up its arms, but it has so far refused amid Israeli cease-fire violations.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger, Adam Rasgon and Aaron Boxerman
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of Hamas in Gaza City in November.

item.title: Nigel Farage, Leader of Reform U.K., Faces Investigation for £5 Million Gift
item.description: The Conservative Party referred the case against Mr. Farage, the leader of a right-wing populist party in Britain, to a parliamentary watchdog.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: Nigel Farage attending the opening of Parliament in London on Wednesday.

item.title: What to Watch at Trump and Xi’s U.S.-China Summit
item.description: Here’s what to know about the biggest sources of tension in U.S.-China relations before the first summit in Beijing in nine years between the nations’ leaders.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport, Ana Swanson and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday.
